entry.title: Re: SAP Add in for C4C - New Outlook
entry.content:
Hi colleague,

The add-in (v4.0 or higher) is only compatible with Classic Outlook and is not supported in the New Outlook for Windows. It’s not a configuration issue  it’s a technical limitation due to Microsoft’s architectural changes. There is no workaround.

If the company wants to continue using the client-side add-in, they will need to stay on Classic Outlook. If they are already moving to modern Microsoft 365, the logical step is to evaluate SAP’s Server-Side Integration, which does not require a local installation and aligns with the new model.

Here are the official references in case you’d like to review them:

Add-in Requirements
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-cloud-for-customer/solution-guide-for-sap-sales-cloud/requirements-for-sap-cloud-for-customer-add-in-for-microsoft-outlook-version-4-0-or-higher

Server-Side Integration
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-cloud-for-customer/solution-guide-for-sap-sales-cloud/sap-cloud-for-customer-server-side-integration-for-microsoft-outlook?locale=en-US

Best regards.
Fernando DJ Espitia.

entry.title: Re: Manage Supplier Line Items- Profit Center is now shwo...
entry.content:
Good day @Sandeep5060 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is a standard behaviour in Manage Supplier Line Items.

What you are seeing is two different situations:

  • In one case, Profit Center shows in line items
  • In another case, Profit Center can be added in the selection/filter, and the supplier line item has a profit center in the underlying accounting, but the profit Center column is blank in the app.

That happens because Manage Supplier Line Items can only display fields that have a one to one relationship to the supplier line item.

Profit Center is stored on the G/L line item level, not on the supplier line item/document header level. So if one supplier invoice has multiple G/L lines and different profit centers, then the app cannot show one single profit center value on the supplier line item row. This scenario is described here: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2901005 and https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3346815

That is why the filed may exist in filters but still not display reliably in the line item result for all documents.

I recommend you use Display Line Items in General Ledger for profit center analysis. The app shows the G/L line items where the Profit Center is actually stored.  This is described in the reference provided above.

See https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2901005 and https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3346815 for more details.

Here is a quick guide:

In your manage supplier line items app,

After executing you may notice some profit centers are blank, which describes

Alternatively, you can see the real Profit Center in accounting line items by using the Display Line Items in General Ledger. You can filter by Supplier, Company Code, Fiscal Year and optionally Journal Entry and execute.

Then display Profit Center, G/L Account etc and you will see Profit Center on the G/L lines

To summarise:

  • The behaviour you see is standard and not a configuration error.
  • Manage Supplier Line Items cannot always display Profit Center because it only shows fields with one-to-one relation to the supplier line item.
  • Profit Center is on G/L line items, so use Display Line Items in General Ledger for reliable reporting.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Service Management [ESM] - How to enable Business Document
entry.content:
Previous Blog : How SAP ESM + Document AI + S/4HANA Work Together to Automate Invoice Processing End‑to‑End

Business Documents

Business Document feature available in SAP Enterprise Service Management SKU (ESM)

Business Documents represent back-end records in a semantic way and can create, update, or display those records. Values in the table can be populated automatically using AI services or entered manually.

Business documents can capture data from email message or case subject and description, attachments of various file types, or using manual data entry. Attachment data and entity information is extracted with generative AI using large language models.

You, as an administrator, can create business document schemas to represent data patterns for different scenarios. Schemas specify which data to use in your business documents and how you want the data formatted in your solution. If you've enabled business documents for a specific case type, you can find the Business Documents tab on the case detail view. Use conditions to make specific business document fields required, read only, or hidden.

Business Document Integration

Create an outbound configuration to enable actions to send or retrieve data from external systems, or to populate values in dropdown menus in business documents based on a dynamic data source.

Prerequisites

Set up an outbound communication system under System Settings -  Integration  - Communication Systems.

Procedure

Go to your user menu and choose System Settings  - Business Documents - Business Document Integration.

  • Choose + (Create).
  • Enter a name and select the communication system.
  • Choose Search and Add to add existing HTTP data outbound configurations or + (Create) to create a new configuration.

Create Business Documents

Select + Create Business Document, Enter the required information, and save

Your new business document appears in the Business Documents list with the header details on the right. A new draft version of the business document appears in the Versions table. Select + Actions to activate the version, copy to a new business document, create new version, or delete the version. There can only be one draft version. You can activate multiple versions but there must only be one active version within the specified validity period.

Select + in the Designer tab to add controls and fields to the business document table.

Add actions to the business document. Go to the Actions tab and select + Add Action.

Go to the Input Options tab to choose one or more input methods for your business document.

Configure Business Document Extraction Schemas

Use schemas to configure document extraction from attachments.

Add Business Document to Case Type

  1. Go to User Menu > System Settings  > All Settings  > Cases  > Case Types.
  2. Select a case type.
  3. Select  (Open in Detail View) to open the case type details.
  4. Switch on Business Documents in the Features Settings section

  1. Choose the Additional Features tab in the case type header.
  2. In the Business Documents section, choose the  (Search) icon to search for a business document to add to this case type.
  3. Choose + (Add) to add the business document to the case type.

    You can assign only one business document to each case type. When a case of this type is created, the Business Documents tab appears in the case detail view.

entry.title: Using AI Agent Cline to Remove Deprecated Code from SAP Fiori Applications
entry.content:
Using AI Agent Cline to Remove Deprecated Code from SAP Fiori Applications

 

Introduction

 

SAP Fiori applications are built on the robust foundation of UI5 libraries, which are updated each quarter to deliver new features and controls. Traditionally, these libraries have evolved with a strong emphasis on backward compatibility, where addressing deprecated code was encouraged but not strictly enforced.

However, with the imminent Fiori UI5 2.0 release, all deprecated elements will be removed, making the elimination of deprecated controls not just a recommendation, but a clear guideline.

In addition, maintaining clean and up-to-date code is crucial for enhancibility, performance, security, and compatibility. Deprecated APIs, outdated libraries, and legacy patterns can lead to technical debt and potential issues in future upgrades.

In this blog, we’ll explore how AI Agent Cline can help identify and remove deprecated code from Fiori applications efficiently.

 

1. Configuring Cline for Fiori Applications

Before using Cline, ensure it is properly configured to analyse SAPUI5/Fiori applications.

Prerequisites

  1. GitHub Copilot license
  2. Visual Studio Code
  3. Installation of GitHub Copilot extension in Visual Studio Code

 

Installation of Cline extension in Visual Studio Code

  • Click on Extension icon (Ctrl+Shift+X)
  • Search for cline
  • Click on install
  • Open Cline by clicking on the Cline icon in the vertical icon toolbar in VS code.

  • The Cline welcome screen is shown.

  • Click on Settings icon in the top right corner

  •  Select GitHub Copilot as the API Provider and copilot -claude-sonnet-4.5 as Language Model and press “Done”.

 

Installation of the MCP server for SAP Fiori

 

  • In the cline panel, click on the MCP Servers icon on the top-right corner.

  • Click on Configure
  • Click on Configure MCP Servers.
  • The file cline_mcp_settings.json opens. Maintain UI5 MCP server as shown below and click on Done.

Note: (@ui5/mcp-server) is a SAP published UI5 MCP server. It allows AI assistants to write SAPUI5 code. SAP currently prevents the usage of MCP servers together with GitHub Copilot. Hence, Cline is being used as an alternative. However a GitHub Copilot license is required for accessing SAP’s approved AI models. UI5 MCP server supports UI5 Linter along with other tools. Linter in particular is useful in identifying deprecated code from the applications and for working on subsequent fixes.

Now that we have configured VS code with all the required tools, we can proceed with fixing the deprecated code issues.

 

2. Prompting Cline to Evaluate Deprecated Code

 

After trying multiple approaches to prompt Cline to evaluate and fix deprecated code following steps proved to give consistent results. This approach is equally effective for free style applications as well as Fiori element based applications.

Tips:

  1. Following example is an approach which worked for my use case. There were apps with ~800 issues. So going slow initially helped me gain confidence in the solution provided by AI. There by reducing subsequent effort in error fixing.
  2. In Cline, describe the task as precisely as possible.
  3. Clone the repository to your workspace
  4. Mention the relevant project in task prompt, if you have multiple repos in your workspace.
  5. Mention that the available MCP servers should be used.
  6. Working context is set in the first prompt and then tasks are executed under it. If a new task is started, then the context is to be set again.
  7. Cline would ask for your approval for using MCP server, for executing certain commands. Auto approval can be configured in MCP configuration.
  8. File changes by Cline can be reviewed in Git Lens view of VS code.
  9. If automation test scripts with good coverage is available in the repository, a better approach will be to fix the deprecated code in test scripts first. These scripts can then be regularly executed by cline to validate changes.

Example:

 

Prompt 1: Setting “Working context”

Cline was asked to find and evaluate deprecated code issues (both error and warning) in the application using linter command. Working context is set for Cline to follow SAP UI5 guidelines only and use the configured UI5 MCP server. This context helps Cline to stay within the SAPUI5 guidelines while solving the issues going forward.

 

Cline would eventually categorize the issues and prepare a roadmap for fixing the same.

In cases with large number of identified issued, which might span over days to fix, Cline might prepare a .md file with following type of details for future reference.v                 

  • Categorisation of the issues into Low Medium and High
  • Issue detail with suggested solution
  • Number of files affected with same issue
  • Effort breakup to fix the issues

  • Testing strategy to mitigate subsequent breakdowns
  • Resource required

  • Risk Assessment

 

Prompt 2: Start Fixing the Issues

 

For start we picked 1 type of issue, and fixed it in all files. Maybe start with a low or medium category. Such issues require less effort, get fixed quickly with negligible breakdown in the application. The number of issues start reducing at a fast pace.

We asked Cline to suggest which issue to start with. It evaluated the list of issues to prepare a suggestion. Developer can choose the fixing strategy and include it in the prompt for Cline to suggest accordingly.

We started with a low priority issue as per suggestion shared by Cline.

For first few files, Cline asked developer to validate the changes before saving. Cline can be instructed to save the file directly.

Once some issues are fixed and tested, you can instruct cline to replicate the fix in other files without approval. Change log can then be viewed in Git Lens.

A summary get generated by Cline for developer to check how far they have come along with the suggestion for the next steps.

Note:

  1. We were able to resolve 200 (low + medium) errors and 200 warnings in 1 PD using this approach.
  2. If your application has custom logic coded in UI. Recommendation would be to break a big controller task into small steps for Cline. This would improve the efficiency of the solution being generated.
  3. Considerable effort reduction can be achieved once initial steps are eased into.
  4. Approximately 30-40% of manual effort is saved with the approach suggested. Once the confidence in AI suggestions improve, the number might be higher.

Tags: #SAPUI5 #Fiori #DeprecatedCode #AI #Cline #SAPDevelopment

entry.title: Replicate SAP S/4HANA Measuring Points and Measurement Documents to SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
In asset-intensive industries, equipment health isn’t just a metric—it’s the backbone of operational efficiency. Organizations running SAP S/4HANA often use Measuring Points and Measurement Documents to monitor equipment performance and condition. These data points help ensure timely maintenance and prevent costly downtime.

But here’s the challenge:
When field technicians work in SAP Field Service Management (FSM), they often lack access to these critical insights. Without real-time data in the field, service teams face delays, reactive maintenance, and frustrated customers.

So, what’s the solution?
Seamless integration between SAP S/4HANA and SAP FSM. By replicating Measuring Points and Measurement Documents from S/4HANA to FSM, technicians gain real-time visibility into asset health—right where the work happens.

Why Does This Integration Matter?

Imagine this scenario:

  • A technician is dispatched to repair a pump.
  • In FSM, they see the service order but have no clue about the pump’s recent performance trends.
  • They spend extra time diagnosing issues that could have been avoided if they had access to measurement readings.

With integration:

  • The technician sees the latest pressure readings, temperature and vibration trends in FSM.
  • They arrive prepared, diagnose faster, and fix proactively.
  • Result? Less downtime, happier customers, and lower costs.

Key Benefits of Integration

  • Real-time synchronization of equipment performance data between S/4HANA and FSM.
  • Improved visibility for technicians in the field.
  • Proactive service delivery, leading to higher customer satisfaction.

Scope of the Integration

Here’s what’s included in the S/4HANA On-Premise October 2025 release.

  • Measuring Point Replication
    • Create or update measuring points in S/4HANA.
    • Changes automatically replicate to FSM for the corresponding equipment.
  • Measurement Document Replication
    • Measurement readings or counters created in S/4HANA replicate to FSM.
    • Readings can also be captured during maintenance order confirmation.
    • Measurement readings captured in FSM replicate back to S/4HANA is available via SAP Note: 3684637 - Implementation of Replication Logic for Measurement Document from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
  • Initial and Delta Loads
    • Supports both bulk initial load and incremental updates.
  • Multi-company Support
    • Data replication can be configured for multiple FSM companies.

Prerequisites

  • For Measuring Points in FSM:
    • Measurement Point Category (handled by integration).
    • Equipment and Characteristics (already managed via MAIF framework).
  • For Measurement Documents in FSM:
    • The corresponding Measuring Point must exist in FSM.

Configuration Steps

Delta Replication

  • For single FSM company:
    Navigate to SPRO → Service → Integration with Other SAP Solutions → SAP Field Service Management → Configure Integration

Maintain following settings (Use the same RFC destination as Equipment replication.):

 

  • For multiple FSM companies:
    SPRO → Service → Integration with Other SAP Solutions → SAP Field Service Management → Configure Multiple Company Replication

Define S/4HANA FSM Company:

 

Assig FSM Company Details:

 

 

Assign Replication Object Details:

 

  • Create BGRFC inbound destination:
    Transaction: SBGRFCCONF

 

 

  • Extract AIF Contents:
    Transaction: /AIF/CONTENT_EXTRACT

 

 

Initial Load

  • Go to DRFIMG → Define Custom Settings for Data Replication → Define Technical Settings for Business Systems.

 

Maintain Business System and Logical System (Ignore if already created):

 

Define Business Systems, BOs:

 

Define Business Systems, Bos, Communication Channel:

 

Maintain the above settings for BO Type 5236 as well.

 

  • Define Replication Models:

Go to DRFIMG -> Define Custom Settings for Data Replication -> Define Replication Models

 

Create following replication models:

 

             

Maintain the following settings for Replication Model MDOC_REPL:

 

 

 

 

 

 

Maintain the abovementioned settings for Replication Model MP_REPL as well.

 

Delta replication Demo

Scenario: Wind energy operator: Each turbine has measuring point for running hours. This points (created on the equipment/functional location in S/4HANA) is replicate to FSM. When maintenance technician performs the service then maintenance measurement readings are captured during maintenance order confirmation. These readings are also replicated to FSM.

Create Measuring Point: tcode: IK01

 

 

Check the measuring point replication log in S/4HANA

Tcode /AIF/ERR

 

 

 

Check the replication in FSM:

Note: Measuring Points and Measurement documents can be viewed with in the equipment using Mobile App. At present it cannot be viewed via FSM website.

 

 

Create a maintenance order for the maintenance of the Wind Turbine and assign the Measuring point as PRT

 

 

Create maintenance order confirmation using tcode: IW41 and create measurement document

 

 

Measurement document can also be viewed in tcode IK13

 

 

Check the measurement document replication log in S/4HANA

Tcode /AIF/ERR

 

 

 

For more details, visit https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/738a456365c0414faba6426d05fd8674/0ccd50bd60554bfe90af11864497f5de.html?locale=en-US.

Final Thoughts

Integrating Measuring Points and Measurement Documents between SAP S/4HANA and FSM isn’t just a technical upgrade—it’s a strategic move toward data-driven field service. With real-time insights, technicians can work smarter, customers stay happier, and businesses save money.

entry.title: Administrator locked from CMC Application
entry.content:
Dear all,

by fault I've denied CMC access for Everyone group in SAP BO 4.3 SP04 Patch14 through CMC --> applications --> Central Management Console --> User Security --> Everyone --> View --> Assign Security --> Add/ Remove Rights --> Log on to the CMC and view this object in the CMC --> denied.

This resulted in even the Administrator user not being able to log into the CMC anymore. Thus there is no user left to access the CMC.

I scanned through the KBAs and found

1751854 - Unable to unrestrict CMC Tab Access in BusinessObjects BI platform 4

<https://me.sap.com/notes/1751854/E>

1797057 - Unable to restrict / unrestrict CMC Tab Access in Business Intelligence 4.0

<https://me.sap.com/notes/1797057/E>

3151174 - "You do not have access rights to log on to the application" error in CMC after trying to log on

<https://me.sap.com/notes/3151174/E>

... and others which sounded promising but left me with no actual solution resolving the problem.

In addition I tried several suggestions by AI but even though those might have worked for older versions it seems SAP BO 4.3 (SP04 Patch14) is unimpressed by the effort. Tried .NET Powershell approach, Java SDK approach as well as an approach through RESTful API with no luck. I've also tried to follow suggestions with the SAP BI Platform Support Tool but those do not seem to be valid anymore too.


May I ask if anyone has experienced the same and found a solution?

Additional environment details:

  • SAP BusinessObjects Version: SAP BO 4.3 SP04 Patch14
  • CMS Database Type: Oracle 19c
  • Operating System: Windows Server 2022 21H2

Thanks a lot in advance.

Kindest regards
Ulf

entry.title: Re: Custom Business Catalog missing in App "Manage Launch...
entry.content:
Good day @abap_learner1 

Thank you for your question.

What you are experiencing is an extensibility setup flow and prerequisites issue. Your scenario is explained in KBA 3491119. Also, for extensibility apps you are required to follow the official activation and business catalog creation flow

Regarding the tutorial you followed, it may be technically valid as a development guide, but the Public Cloud launchpad exposure depends on SAP’s supported activation/catalog/role flow in your tenant and release. If you skip one of those steps or you do not fulfil a prerequisite, that can lead to the “IAM app visible, catalog not resolvable” behaviour you experienced.

The approach I recommend is:

  • Confirm prerequisite scope item is active
  • Verify in ADT/BAS deployment flow that you activated the app in the same tenant where you are testing Manage Launchpad Pages. You need to ensure:
    • Corrects system (dev/customising tenant)
    • App activation completed successfully
    • IAM app was successfully created
    • No activation errors in your deployment logs
  • In your custom business catalog, confirm the IAM app is listed and open the app entry to verify it resolves correctly. If it still shows errors, then it points to incomplete backend catalog generation, or you are missing a prerequisite setup. Also mentioned here KBA 3491119.
  • Do not rely only on the IAM app visibility in Display IAM Apps. Follow the developer extensibility steps.
  • Assign the catalog to a business role (the role must be assigned to your user) and republish launchpad content. You should then add the app tile to the Manage Launchpad Pages

  • If it still fails after those steps and the error persists, I suggest you raise an incident.

Note that the symptom can also be caused by backend catalog generation inconsistencies, which is also addressed in KBA 3491119

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Laravel Shared Hosting Issue — Serving Uploaded Images Without Symlinks
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m deploying a Laravel + Livewire application on shared hosting and running into a frustrating issue with serving uploaded images. Everything works great on localhost, but once pushed live, image assets don’t load in Blade templates.

What’s Happening

  • Uploaded images are stored in storage/app/public/ads/content.

  • On the live server, .AVIF images appear in generated previews, but .JPG and .PNG images do not load at all.

  • Calls like {{ asset('storage/ads/content/'.$imageName) }} produce broken links everywhere.

  • Running php artisan storage:link fails with a 500 Server Error because my hosting provider does not allow symlink().

I’ve already tried:

  • Updating APP_URL and clearing config cache

  • Converting images to Base64 (which helps only for API previews, not for served images)

It feels like the storage path is there — like pieces in a Letter Boxed puzzle — but Laravel can’t “connect” the storage files to the public path due to missing symlinks.

My Questions

  1. Has anyone figured out how to serve storage images on shared hosting without symlinks in Laravel?

  2. Is there a reliable pattern to make asset('storage/...') point to the correct physical path?

  3. Are there recommended workarounds for shared hosting restrictions when serving public assets?

Goal

I’d like the site to:

Serve user-uploaded .JPG, .PNG, and other images correctly

Avoid relying on symlinks (since they’re blocked

Work on a standard shared hosting environment

If anyone has solved this type of issue before (or knows a clean pattern), I’d really appreciate your advice.

Thanks in advance! 😊

entry.title: Re: Unable to extract S_ALR reports output to RFC
entry.content:
GRR1 reports are built on Report Writer (table T811) *.

Instead of calling S_ALR transaction, you can:

  • Identify report name (table TKEB or GR55 assignment).
  • Use Function modules:
    • GRW_REPORT_EXECUTE
    • GRW_JOB_SUBMIT
  • Extract the ALV internal table programmatically.
  • Wrap the result in a custom RFC-enabled FM
  • Consume via BTP.

This avoids screen-based execution.

entry.title: What's New in SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Business Uses and Configuration
entry.content:
1. Product Availability Check (PAC) Enhancements in SAP S/4HANA 2025

1.1 Flexible Reservation & Locking Strategies

PAC now supports three locking approaches:

What’s new: PAC now supports three locking approaches:

  • Optimistic reservation
    • Temporary confirmation without immediate DB lock
    • Lock applied only during final commit
    • Improves performance for high-volume order entry
  • Exclusive locking
    • Locks supply immediately during check
    • Prevents parallel confirmations consuming same stock
    • Best for scarce or regulated inventory
  • Automatic selection
    • System dynamically chooses locking logic

Business scenario example:

  • E-commerce bulk orders → Optimistic locking improves response time
  • Aerospace spare parts → Exclusive locking ensures accuracy

Configuration steps:

SPRO → Sales and Distribution → Advanced ATP → Product Availability Check → Configure Reservation / Locking

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Configure Product Availability Check
  • Check Product Availability
  • Review Availability Check Result

1.2 Enhanced Scope of Check Logic

Bucket Logic Options

  • Progressive → confirms earliest feasible supply (may split dates)
  • Conservative → confirms only if full quantity available

Check Without Batch

ATP ignores batch segmentation during check.

When it’s useful:

  • Batch assigned later by warehouse
  • Quality inspection determines batch
  • Batch irrelevant at order stage

Subcontracting Supply/Demand Handling

PAC now better accounts for:

  • Components issued to subcontractor
  • Expected finished-goods receipt

Configuration steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Product Availability Check → Define Scope of Check

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Configure Product Availability Check
  • Check Product Availability

1.3 Horizon for Late Supply

What it does: If supply arrives later than expected:

  • Confirmation can shift automatically
  • Within configured tolerance window

Prevents unnecessary:

  • Cancellation
  • Rescheduling
  • Customer communication noise

Example:

Production delay = 2 days
Tolerance horizon = 3 days
Confirmation auto-adjusted

Configuration steps

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Product Availability Check → Maintain Check Horizon

1.4 Automatic Quantity Rounding

New behavior in SAP S/4HANA 2025:

Confirmed quantities validated against:

  • Delivery unit
  • Packaging rules
  • Rounding profile

Example:

Order: 97 units
Delivery unit: 10
Confirmation: 90 units

Configuration steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Product Availability Check → Maintain Rounding Rules

2. Backorder Processing (BOP) Enhancements in SAP S/4HANA 2025

2.1 Availability Change Log (ACL)

What’s new - System logs every event affecting availability:

  • Goods receipts
  • Production completion
  • Purchase order updates
  • Allocation changes
  • Supply protection updates

Result - BOP now:

  • Reprocesses only impacted orders
  • Runs faster
  • Supports near-real-time prioritization

Configuration steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Backorder Processing → Activate Availability Change Log

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Backorder Processing
  • Backorder Processing Monitor

2.2 Event-Driven Availability Updates (RAP Integration)

aATP now aligns with modern ABAP RESTful event handling:

  • Cleaner extensibility model
  • Supports automation workflows
  • Enables responsive ATP recalculation

Useful for:

  • Custom logic
  • Cloud extensions
  • API-driven processes

2.3 Interactive Backorder Processing Improvements

Major capabilities - Business can now:

  • Drag-and-drop reorder priorities
  • Upload worklists
  • Override allocation/supply protection temporarily
  • Fix confirmation date or quantity
  • Process orders by segment

Business value - Planner can:

  • Instantly prioritize VIP customer
  • Reclaim supply from low-priority order
  • Confirm urgent shipment in seconds

Configuration Steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Backorder Processing → Define BOP Segments & Strategies

Fiori Apps applicable:

  • Manage Confirmations for Backorders

3. Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC) Enhancements in SAP S/4HANA 2025

3.1 Storage Location Constraint

ABC strategy can now enforce:

  • Confirmation must consider original storage location or
  • Restrict substitution to allowed locations

Useful for:

  • Regulated goods
  • Ownership-based stock
  • Quality-specific inventory

3.2 Storage Location Constraint New Rating Attributes

Maximum Sequence Number: Limits number of alternative sources checked.

Minimum Consumption Priority: Ensures preferred sources used first.

Benefit - Improves:

  • Performance
  • Sourcing efficiency
  • Predictability

3.3 Maximum Partial Deliveries Control

System respects limit on: number of partial shipments

Avoids:

  • Excessive delivery fragmentation
  • Logistics complexity

3.4 Enhanced Logging & BAdI

Logging: Improved traceability in SLG1.

BAdI: BADI_ATP_ABC_ENRCD_SUBSTN_RSLT

Allows:

  • Dynamic substitution logic
  • Attribute modification

Configuration Steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Alternative-Based Confirmation → Maintain Strategies

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Configure Alternative-Based Confirmation
  • Review Availability Check Result

4. Third-Party Order Processing Integration in SAP S/4HANA 2025

4.1 Availability-Controlled Purchasing

ABC can now dynamically do the following:

If internal supply insufficient → evaluate vendor availability → confirm via third-party sourcing.

Supported scenarios

  • Vendor → Customer (drop-ship)
  • Vendor → Internal plant → Customer

4.2 Backorder Follow-Up Strategies

Shortage quantity can automatically and reduces manual interventions:

  • Trigger supplier sourcing
  • Convert into third-party fulfillment

4.3 Unified Confirmation Visibility

Third-party confirmations appear in: Review Availability Check Result

Business sees:

  • Internal supply result
  • Supplier confirmation
  • Substitution logic

4.4 Business Process Scheduling Integration

Custom scheduling schemas allow:

  • Supplier-specific lead time calculation
  • Dynamic ETA determination

Configuration steps:

SPRO → Cross-Application Components → Business Process Scheduling

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Review Availability Check Result
  • Display Sales Order Fulfillment Issues

5. Automatic STO Creation (Plant Consolidation) in SAP S/4HANA 2025

5.1 How it works in SAP S/4HANA 2025

If: Supplying plant ≠ Customer-facing plant

ABC can:

  • Create Stock Transport Order automatically
  • Move stock internally
  • Confirm delivery from customer plant

Business impact

Without consolidation:

  • Multiple plants ship separately

With consolidation:

  • Single shipment
  • Reduced freight cost
  • Simplified delivery process

Configuration steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Alternative-Based Confirmation → Configure Plant Substitution / STO Creation

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Review Availability Check Result
  • Manage Stock Transfer Orders
  • Manage Sales Orders

6. Product Allocation (PAL) in SAP S/4HANA 2025

6.1 Flexible Allocation Periods

Previously

Rigid weekly/monthly buckets.

Now

You can:

  • Create any duration period
  • Auto-generate period sequences
  • Upload/download planning data
  • Migrate existing objects

Business Example:

  • 5-day launch period
  • 12-day seasonal period

Configuration Steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Product Allocation → Maintain Allocation Objects & Period Types

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Manage Product Allocation Planning Data
  • Monitor Product Allocation

6.2 CDS Extraction Views for Analytics

Planning Data available in:

I_PRODALLOCOBJTIMESERIESDEX

Consumption Data available in:

I_PRODALLOCQTYASSIGNMENTDEX

Enables below view:

  • SAC dashboards
  • Predictive analysis
  • Custom reporting

7. Supply Protection (SUP) in SAP S/4HANA 2025

7.1 Resolve Overprotection App

Problem addressed: Protection rules can unintentionally block all confirmations, resulting in no quantities being confirmed for sales or delivery requests.

The new app helps you proactively identify issues by detecting:
• Protection objects that are fully blocked and preventing confirmations
• Conflicting or overlapping protection rules causing unintended restrictions
• Situations that could lead to zero confirmations, impacting order fulfillment and customer commitments

7.2 Automated Mitigation Suggestions

Priority-based protection reduction: The system evaluates protection hierarchy and rule priority in aATP and proposes decreasing protected quantities at selected priority levels to resolve confirmation shortages while maintaining higher-priority allocations.

Lowest-segment protection optimization: Identifies protection segments (customer/channel/region) with surplus reserved stock and recommends reducing protection in the lowest-priority segment to release supply with minimal impact on strategic demand.

Restricted-quantity recalibration: Analyzes protection object limits and confirmation logic, suggesting adjustments to restricted quantities or release thresholds so that available stock can participate in ATP checks and avoid zero-confirmation outcomes.

7.3 Situation Management Alerts

Automatic planner notification: When aATP detects a supply–demand imbalance (for example, protection-rule conflicts, shortages, or zero-confirmation risk), a Situation Management alert is generated and automatically assigned to the responsible planner via the situation template and recipient determination.

Event-driven alert creation: The system monitors predefined ATP-relevant conditions and thresholds; once violated, it creates a situation instance with contextual data (product, location, order, confirmation status) for immediate analysis and action.

Workflow-triggered response: The situation can automatically trigger follow-up workflows—such as task creation, email/Fiori notification, or escalation—enabling planners to review protection settings, adjust allocations, or re-run confirmation checks without manual monitoring.

Configuration Steps:

SPRO → SD → Advanced ATP → Supply Protection → Maintain Protection Objects & Groups

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Resolve Overprotection
  • Monitor Supply Protection

8. Supply-Creation Based Confirmation in SAP S/4HANA 2025

8.1 Length-Based ATP

Functionality

  • System can confirm orders using longer available material, planning cutting/splitting to meet requested length.
  • Tolerance rules allow acceptable over-length or deviation during confirmation.
  • Supports automatic feasible confirmation instead of rejection when exact length stock is unavailable.
  • Helps optimize material utilization, reduce scrap, and minimize manual planner intervention.

Delivered Technical Objects

  • Planning Procedure: 7 – activates length-oriented planning logic in PP/DS.
  • Heuristic: SAP_LEN_001N – standard heuristic for:
    • Length-based planning
    • Cut optimization scenarios
    • Tolerance-based confirmations
    • Allocation of variable-dimension stock

Configuration steps

  • SPRO → Production Planning → PP/DS → Define Planning Procedures & Heuristics

8.2 Characteristics-Based Availability Check

Integration Capabilities

  • ATP triggers PP/DS-based evaluation to validate:
    • Configuration characteristics – ensures selected variant or options are manufacturable.
    • Production feasibility – checks capacity, resources, and material availability in detailed scheduling.
    • Optional supply creation – system can automatically generate planned orders or procurement proposals if supply is missing.

Delivered Technical Objects

  • Planning Procedure: 6 – enables PP/DS-supported supply check during ATP confirmation.
  • Heuristic: SAP_PP_SBC_R – used for:
    • Supply-based confirmation logic
    • Automatic planned order creation
    • Feasibility-oriented production scheduling

Fiori apps applicable:

  • Check Product Availability
  • Schedule Production
  • Manage Planned Orders

Conclusion:

I would say, SAP S/4HANA 2025 aATP release delivers meaningful technical enhancements that improve the robustness and intelligence of the order confirmation process across planning, allocation, and execution layers:

  • Automation → Automated STO creation and refined sourcing logic improve supply selection, reduce manual intervention, and ensure consistent confirmation behavior during ATP checks.
  • Control → Enhancements to Interactive BOP and overprotection resolution provide finer control over confirmation redistribution, enabling planners to rebalance supply based on priority, rules, and real-time availability.
  • Flexibility → Extended allocation-period handling and enriched substitution attributes allow more granular product/location substitution and time-phased protection management aligned with business constraints.
  • Manufacturing integration → PP-based ATP improvements strengthen the linkage between production supply (planned/process orders, receipts, capacity-relevant dates) and confirmation logic, resulting in more accurate commit dates.

From a system and process perspective, these capabilities help organizations with complex, multi-node fulfillment networks achieve:

  • More deterministic and reliable confirmations through improved rule evaluation and supply matching
  • Increased real-time supply transparency across allocations, protection objects, and production receipts
  • Higher planning and logistics efficiency via reduced manual BOP runs and exception handling
  • Improved service levels and customer promise accuracy driven by tighter integration between ATP, allocation, and manufacturing data

Overall, the 2025 release continues the evolution of aATP toward a proactive, exception-driven confirmation framework, enabling supply chain teams to move from reactive troubleshooting to controlled, automated order promising.

If you want to configuration steps, you will get the detail step by step configuration of SAP S/4HANA 2025 aATP on SAP help  - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f132c385e0234fe68ae9ff35b2da178c/a004ec57a7b5bc12e10000000a4450e5.html 

Also you can read summary of changes in SAP S/4HANA 2025 aATP written by Sujeet Acharya (SAP Product Lead) -  https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/release-highlights-of-advanced-available-to-promise-aatp-in-sap-cloud-erp/ba-p/14240423

entry.title: Re: SAP Event Mesh (EMIS): When to Use AMQP vs MQTT vs REST for S/4HANA Eventing
entry.content:
In EMIS/Event Mesh, the protocol choice mainly depends on the consumer type.

For your scenario, (S/4 as produce and CPI or 3rd part as consumer):

  • AMQP -> Recommended for CPI and enterprise backend integrations. It supports durable queues, acknowledges, and reliable delivery. This is the standard approach for S/4 -> Event Mesh -> CPI.
  • MQTT -> Best suited for lightweight or LoT-Style consumers. Not typically used for CPI-based enterprise integrations.
  • REST -> Mainly for management APIs or simple HTTP-based interactions. Noy ideal for high-volume event consumption. 

So, in your case, if CPI is consuming events from Event Mesh, AMQP would be the right choice.

entry.title: Re: SAP Add in for C4C - New Outlook
entry.content:
Hi colleague,

The add-in (v4.0 or higher) is only compatible with Classic Outlook and is not supported in the New Outlook for Windows. It’s not a configuration issue — it’s a technical limitation due to Microsoft’s architectural changes. There is no workaround.

If the company wants to continue using the client-side add-in, they will need to stay on Classic Outlook. If they are already moving to modern Microsoft 365, the logical step is to evaluate SAP’s Server-Side Integration, which does not require a local installation and aligns with the new model.

Here are the official references in case you’d like to review them:

Add-in Requirements
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-cloud-for-customer/solution-guide-for-sap-sales-cloud/requirements-for-sap-cloud-for-customer-add-in-for-microsoft-outlook-version-4-0-or-higher

Server-Side Integration
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-cloud-for-customer/solution-guide-for-sap-sales-cloud/sap-cloud-for-customer-server-side-integration-for-microsoft-outlook?locale=en-US

Best regards.
Fernando DJ Espitia.

entry.title: 🇩🇪 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Herne, Germany)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, the new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool, so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM, and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio – such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what after all is an LLM.

Currently, the event is based on these tutorials: Joule Studio CodeJam

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, April 14, 2026 from 09:00 - 15:00 local time in Herne, Germany (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: rku.it GmbH, Berliner Platz 9, Herne, Germany

  • The event is kindly being hosted by rku – THANK YOU!
  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocates @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement Reconciliation
entry.content:
Hi,

I believe you need to check the settings in T-code: OT83 under the posting rules configuration, you make sure the posting type is set to “8 – Outgoing G/L Clearing.” Once this is configured, the system will automatically clear the outgoing G/L account when you run FF_5. No need to run a separate transaction to clear the outgoing G/L account manually.

Regards Anwar

entry.title: SAP Databricks – Best Practices SQL Query Performance Tuning Tips
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will share few Best practices guides you query performance tuning tips that one of your Data Analysts and other SQL users can apply to improve DBSQL Serverless performance.

In below suggested tips using one of the integrated landscape - SAP Datasphere integrated with SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Databricks

Choose the Right Warehouse Size & Enable Autoscaling

The Databricks SQL Serverless engine powers SAP Databricks compute. Selecting the right warehouse size (S, M, L, etc.) and autoscaling policies ensures your queries have adequate compute without over‑provisioning. Proper resource optimization is foundational to performance.

Optimize Storage Using Delta Lake

Under the hood, SAP Databricks uses Databricks’ optimized open storage formats. By using Delta Lake, your queries benefit from columnar storage, data skipping, ACID transactions, and optimized IO pathways—dramatically boosting speed.

Avoid SELECT *

SELECT * FROM my_table will retrieve all the columns and rows from the given table and it is an Input-Output intensive operation for a large table.

Scanning unnecessary columns slows queries and inflates compute costs. Always specify the exact columns you need—especially when querying wide SAP analytical datasets.

Integer vs String Data Types

Use integer and not to use String on Primary Key

Optimize Data Layout (Statistics, OPTIMIZE, VACUUM)

Three essential housekeeping operations:

  • ANALYZE TABLE collects table statistics for better planning.
  • OPTIMIZE compacts small files and organizes data.
  • VACUUM cleans unused files to improve scan speed.

These drastically improve performance, especially with frequently refreshed SAP operational data.

ANALYZE collects the statistics of a specified table. These statistics are used by the DBSQL query optimiser to generate a better execution plan.

ANALYZE TABLE demo_19538.cashflow_dp.zcashflow COMPUTE STATISTICS;

OPTIMIZE statement optimizes the layout of the Delta Lake data files. Optionally you can Z-Order the data to colocate the related data in the same set of files.

OPTIMIZE demo_19538.cashflow_dp.zcashflow;
OPTIMIZE demo_19538.cashflow_dp.zcashflow ZORDER BY (COMPANYCODE);

VACUUM removes unused files from the table directory. It removed all the data files that are no longer in the latest state of the transaction log for the table and are older than a retention threshold. The default threshold is 7 days. If you run Vacuum on a delta table, you lose the ability to time travel back to the older versions of the table but it helps in reducing the storage cost.

VACUUM demo_19538.cashflow_dp.zcashflow DRY RUN;
VACUUM demo_19538.cashflow_dp.zcashflow;

Use Query Profiles & EXPLAIN Plans

Databricks provides rich diagnostics:

  • Query Profile highlights slow operators, joins, and shuffles.
  • EXPLAIN FORMATTED shows the logical and physical execution plan.

Use these tools regularly to spot bottlenecks in SAP data models and adjust your SQL accordingly

It is very important to explore and analyse your query plan before executing it. This enables you to understand how the code will actually be executed and is useful for optimising your queries. The DBSQL optimiser automatically generates the most optimised physical plan which is then executed.

EXPLAIN 
SELECT
COMPANYCODE, count(CASHFLOW) AS TotalTrip
FROM
demo_19538.cashflow_dp.zcashflow
GROUP BY COMPANYCODE;

Reduce Shuffle Volume with Better Partitioning

Partition SAP datasets carefully:

  • Avoid too many small partitions.
  • Avoid over‑partitioning tables under 1TB.
  • Use partitioning only when your access patterns require it.

This reduces shuffle operations and speeds aggregation-heavy SAP reporting queries.

Use Caching Strategically

Caching can cut repeated query runtimes by 50–70%, especially in BI scenarios. Databricks caching options include:

  • Delta Cache on SSD-backed nodes
  • DataFrame caching (cache() / persist())

Monitor cache hit rates and pre‑warm caches for SAP dashboards.

entry.title: Re: SendKeys from VBA to a session window
entry.content:
Hi Stefan,

I'm trying to solve a problem and came across your post!

I need to copy the operation text from a service order in SAP transaction IW32, but so far I haven't been able to find a way to do it.

The way I think would work is the SendKeys "^a" command. I used the commands from your post but it's not working!

Could you guide me on the correct way to use these commands?

My code is below:

Option Explicit

Private Declare PtrSafe Function SetForegroundWindow Lib "user32.dll" _
(ByVal hWnd As Long) As Long

Sub getTextOperation()
Dim Appl, SapGuiAuto, Session, Connection, WScript
Dim wsh
Dim hWnd

Set wsh = CreateObject("WScript.Shell")

If Not IsObject(Appl) Then
Set SapGuiAuto = GetObject("SAPGUI")
Set Appl = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine
End If
If Not IsObject(Connection) Then
Set Connection = Appl.Children(0)
End If
If Not IsObject(Session) Then
Set Session = Connection.Children(0)
End If
If IsObject(WScript) Then
WScript.ConnectObject Session, "on"
WScript.ConnectObject Appl, "on"
End If

Session.starttransaction ("IW32")

'Enter the work order and access the operation text
Session.findById("wnd[0]").maximize
Session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtCAUFVD-AUFNR").Text = "105679148"
Session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
Session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subSUB_ALL:SAPLCOIH:3001/ssubSUB_LEVEL:SAPLCOIH:1100/tabsTS_1100/tabpIHKZ/ssubSUB_AUFTRAG:SAPLCOIH:1120/subAVO:SAPLCOI0:0310/btnLTICON-LTOPR").press

hWnd = Session.ActiveWindow.Handle
SetForegroundWindow hWnd

wsh.AppActivate ("SAP Logon for Windows")

'selects all text from the operation
wsh.SendKeys "^a"

'Copy the operation text
Session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[38]").press

End Sub

entry.title: Performance UPDATE SET WHERE vs FROM TABLE with HANA database
entry.content:
I've got a database table, I want to update one column (among tens) of 10.000 lines, what is faster among these 3 possibilities:

  • UPDATE dbtab FROM TABLE itab
  • UPDATE dbtab FROM TABLE itab INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE set_ind
  • UPDATE dbtab SET column = value WHERE (primary_key_condition_in_static_form) repeated for each line

NB: I'm deliberately omitting the solution based on an updatable database view containing only the primary key fields and the column to update via UPDATE FROM TABLE (which would be faster than all the cases above).

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Performance UPDATE SET WHERE vs FROM TABLE with HANA database
entry.content:
Performance test for updating one CHAR 255 column in all 10.000 lines of a database table with column store and load unit Column Preferred, in an ABAP 7.58 SP 1, / HANA 2.00.076 system:

  • Faster: UPDATE dbtab FROM TABLE itab = 0.28 seconds
  • UPDATE dbtab FROM TABLE itab INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ind = 0.41 seconds
  • UPDATE dbtab SET column = value WHERE (primary_key_condition) * 10.000 = 7.38 seconds

Test code:

REPORT zdemo.

CLASS ltc_app DEFINITION FINAL
  FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS update_set_where               FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.
    METHODS update_from_table              FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.
    METHODS update_from_table_with_indicat FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS ltc_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD update_set_where.
    DATA table_zdbtab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zdbtab.

    SELECT *
      FROM zdbtab
      INTO TABLE @table_zdbtab.
    LOOP AT table_zdbtab REFERENCE INTO DATA(ls_zdbtab).
      UPDATE zdbtab
      SET text_column = 'Test'
      WHERE bukrs = ls_zdbtab->bukrs
        AND belnr = ls_zdbtab->belnr
        AND gjahr = ls_zdbtab->gjahr.
    ENDLOOP.
    COMMIT WORK.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update_from_table.
    DATA table_zdbtab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zdbtab.

    SELECT *
      FROM zdbtab
      INTO TABLE @table_zdbtab.
    LOOP AT table_zdbtab REFERENCE INTO DATA(ls_zdbtab).
      ls_zdbtab->text_column = 'Test'.
    ENDLOOP.
    UPDATE zdbtab FROM TABLE @table_zdbtab.
    COMMIT WORK.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD update_from_table_with_indicat.
    TYPES ty_zdbtab TYPE zdbtab WITH INDICATORS ind.
    DATA table_zdbtab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_zdbtab.

    SELECT *
      FROM zdbtab
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @table_zdbtab.
    LOOP AT table_zdbtab REFERENCE INTO DATA(ls_zdbtab).
      ls_zdbtab->text_column = 'Test'.
      ls_zdbtab->ind-text_column = '01'.
    ENDLOOP.
    UPDATE zdbtab FROM TABLE @table_zdbtab INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ind.
    COMMIT WORK.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect iOS Icon & Splash Screen Assets
entry.content:
Solved: 

For some reasons Assets.car inside the app payload is showing depreciated image assets even if Assets.xcassets has the correct files.

Assets.car

solution: before self-resigning, recompile Assets.xcassets

xcrun actool "$HOME/Downloads/self-sign/Assets.xcassets" \
--compile "$HOME/Downloads/self-sign/ipa/Payload/RNVApp.app" \
--platform iphoneos \
--minimum-deployment-target 15.5 \
--target-device iphone \
--target-device ipad \
--app-icon AppIcon \
--output-partial-info-plist "$HOME/Downloads/self-sign/asset-info.plist"

Then merge the generated plist 

/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c "Merge $HOME/Downloads/self-sign/asset-info.plist" \
"$HOME/Downloads/self-sign/ipa/Payload/RNVApp.app/Info.plist"

This should generate a correct Assets.car file and you should be able to continue to self-resign as usual, The correct image icons should now show in the iOS app. Hope this helps.

VG

entry.title: What HANA native SQL query is behind "UPDATE dbtab FROM TABLE itab INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ind"
entry.content:
I'm using this code to update or not some columns in the database table ZSCARR, which is a copy of SCARR, by using "UPDATE ... INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ...":

REPORT zdemo.
TYPES type_line_of_zscarr TYPE zscarr WITH INDICATORS ind.
DATA itab TYPE TABLE OF type_line_of_zscarr.

SELECT *
  FROM zscarr
	INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @itab.

LOOP AT tab REFERENCE INTO DATA(line).
  IF condition. " not given to simplify the question
	  line->url = ''. " it may vary in the real world
	  line->ind-url = '01'.
	ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

UPDATE zscarr FROM TABLE itab INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE ind.
COMMIT WORK.

What is the corresponding HANA native SQL UPDATE?

Thanks.

Sandra

NB: my system is ABAP 7.58 SP 1 and HANA 2.00

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Fan Shop
entry.content: Is there a North America/USA-specific shop?

entry.title: Re: What HANA native SQL query is behind "UPDATE dbtab FROM TABLE itab INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE
entry.content:
The SQL trace (ST05) shows what happens (NB: it runs in the client 100). Here, for a system ABAP 7.58 SP 1 and HANA 2.00:

MERGE /* FDA WRITE */ INTO "ZSCARR" "DBTAB"
USING ITAB[18x7(578)] "ITAB"
("MANDT" NVARCHAR(3),
 "CARRID" NVARCHAR(3),
 "CARRNAME" NVARCHAR(20),
 "CURRCODE" NVARCHAR(5),
 "URL" NVARCHAR(255),
 "IND-CARRNAME" SMALLINT,
 "IND-CURRCODE" SMALLINT,
 "IND-URL" SMALLINT)
ON "DBTAB"."MANDT" = '100' 
AND "DBTAB"."CARRID" = "ITAB"."CARRID"
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET 
"DBTAB"."CARRNAME" = CASE "ITAB"."IND-CARRNAME" WHEN 1 THEN "ITAB"."CARRNAME" ELSE "DBTAB"."CARRNAME" END,
"DBTAB"."CURRCODE" = CASE "ITAB"."IND-CURRCODE" WHEN 1 THEN "ITAB"."CURRCODE" ELSE "DBTAB"."CURRCODE" END,
"DBTAB"."URL"      = CASE "ITAB"."IND-URL"      WHEN 1 THEN "ITAB"."URL"      ELSE "DBTAB"."URL" END

If the type was defined via "WITH INDICATORS ind TYPE abap_bool" (character indicators instead of default byte indicators), the "IND-*" columns would be "NVARCHAR(1)" instead of "SMALLINT" and the update condition would be "WHEN N'X' THEN" instead of "WHEN 1 THEN".

After "UPDATE ... INDICATORS ...", sy-dbcnt is set to the total number of lines in the internal table which have one corresponding line in the database table. Any line of the internal table which has no indicator set counts as one updated line, although technically speaking the values of its columns are not changed.

You may get more information about MERGE INTO in this page of the SAP Help Portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4fe29514fd584807ac9f2a04f6754767/3226201f95764a57810dd256c9524d56.html?locale=en-US.
The page path is SAP HANA Platform > SAP HANA SQL Reference Guide for SAP HANA Platform > SQL Reference > SQL Statements > Alphabetical List of Statements > MERGE INTO Statement (Data Manipulation).

entry.title: Is there enhancement to stop creating inspection lot or make it "skipped" in case of returning quant
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

I've been assigned to task which a little bit tricky because I can't find a suitable enhancement to get it done, I'll describe the issue in detail.

My QM functional consultant requirement is to prevent creating inspection lot or make inspection lot status 'Skipped' depending on specific storage locations without stopping the end user transaction, in case of returning some quantity using Sales document of type returning, through VA02 transaction.
=================================
Cycle Process Steps (for demonstration):
1- Va02 => enter Sales document number
2- Press Deliver

3- Enter how many quantity will be returned, then press Save==================================
Enhancements I checked: 
1- Customer exit (QAAT0001) => didn't been called at any point through all steps.
2- QPL1_INSPLOT_CHANGE => It's called once I click on Deliver, but I don't have the ability to change the inspection lot, in addition to the storage location that I should validate existed in the next step after clicking on deliver.
3- QPAP_FLEX_PLAN => It's called once I click on Save, but If I raise error here, in case of specific storage location, the end-user won't be able to continue the transaction process which isn't the requirement.

So my question: Is there any way "other enhancement" to prevent creating inspection lot or make its status 'Skipped' depending on specific storage location after pressing Save?

Thank you.

entry.title: Settlement Process for Lump Sum and Slotting Fee using CCM.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
 
I am currently working on an SAP implementation project for a group of companies operating manufacturing plants and distribution centers across the GCC region.
 
As part of the business model, our products are distributed to customers (stores/malls) within designated territories, and the company is required to manage, accrue, and subsequently settle the following commercial arrangements through credit memos issued to customers:
 
1. Account Opening Fee – Applicable when registering a new customer or opening a new store.( This is a credit Note which company issuing to the Customer in Legacy system through a Custom Fiori App.)
2. Listing Fee – Charged for launching new products into the market.
3. Monthly Shelf Rental Fee – For racks and shelf space allocated to display company products.
4. Promotional Discounts – Seasonal or festival-related trade promotions.
5. Other Fees – Including support fees, renovation contributions, e-commerce fees, flyer fees, etc.
 
These payments need to pay back to Customer as a Credit Memo, which will adjust/reflect in Customer Statement of accounts. 
 
I would greatly appreciate insights from SMEs who have handled similar scenarios, particularly regarding:
 
• Best practice process design
• Recommended SAP solution approach (standard vs. custom)
• Accounting and commercial treatment
• Use of rebate/condition contract management or alternative frameworks.
 
Looking forward to your valuable suggestions and experiences.
 
PS: The Settlement needs to be posted manually through a Credit Note/Credit memo Request. 
 
Thank you in advance for your support.
Best Regards,
Bharath Katukam

entry.title: An Introduction of the End-2-End Process in Universal Revenue Recognition with Solution Order
entry.content:
Before You Read 

You shall get yourself familiar with the business process of Order-to-Cash, as this blog is focusing on the accounting requirement and process.  

Also you shall understand how event-based revenue recognition works, and the following blog will be helpful for you to start with https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/an-introduction-to-event-based-revenue-recognition-with-customer-projects/ba-p/13410880 

If you are brand-new to URR, a quick introduction of this blog written by Volkmar Zahnwill also be helpful 

While you are reading the blog, you may also read how URR is configured, and you can real the following blog in parallel.https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/universal-revenue-recognition-urr-configuration-walkthrough-release-2602/ba-p/14314320

Recap of URR - Not a New Solution, but a New Level of Unity (Done) 

Universal Revenue Recognition (URR) brings together the strengths of EventBased Revenue Recognition (EBRR) and ContractBased Revenue Recognition (CBRR) so that solution business models—spanning sales, services, projects, and subscriptions—are accounted for consistently. With Release 2602, the Solution Order acts as the commercial “container,” while URR delivers the unified revenue lifecycle behind the scenes. 

In this deep dive, we walk through the full story: how a Solution Order is set up, how operational events trigger realtime revenue postings, how periodend processing completes the picture, and how accountants and revenue managers read the results. 

URR is an overarching concept—one that brings EventBased Revenue Recognition (EBRR) and ContractBased Revenue Recognition (CBRR) into a unified framework. 

Therefore, starting of release 2602, you don’t need to activate URR, rather activate the different scope items of EBRR and CBRR.  

There is also no additional license for using EBRR and CBRR, it is part of the S/4 Public Cloud ERP. 

 

Why and when you need URR? 

In solution business, we rarely sell a single thing. A typical deal might include: 

  • A product shipped from stock 
  • A service contract to provide recurring services 
  • A customer fixedprice project provide consulting service or implementation service 
  • A subscription for recuring billing 

Operationally, these are different worlds; From accounting perspective and according to IFRS 15/ASC 606, , they must come together – A revenue contract with customer.  URR solves exactly this: It uses the solution order as the bundling object to combine different offering in a solution to meet the accounting requirements from IFRS 15/ASC606 . 

Understanding the overall concept 

A Solution Order is the commercial hub that brings together multiple products and services 

  • Sales Order Item for Sell from Stock Scenario 
  • Customer Project for fixed price) 
  • Service Contract Item for recurring services 
  • Subscription Billing Item for recurring subscription)

 

EBRR is always the starting point. In the above diagram, you will see that you first need to define the revenue recognition key in event-based revenue recognition.  This provides you the fundamental features for revenue recognition such as 

  • Real Time Revenue Recognition and Revenue Adjustment 
  • Cost Object Controlling Integration 
  • Margin Analysis 

CBRR is optional, but it requires the relevant scope items of EBRR are activated. CBRR provides a revenue contract management, which creates business objects of revenue contracts and performance obligations. Only after that does CBRR come into play to handle multielement arrangements, revenue contract combination or more complex IFRS15/ASC 606 requirement such as prospective contract modification 

End-2-End Process Overview with Solution Order and URR (Done) 

To demonstrate how URR works in practice, we prepared a comprehensive solution order containing all the above processes. In real business, you may choose some of those processes. 

The E2E Process consists of 3 major parts: 

  • Solution Order Setup: You will see how a revenue contract is created and what additional information is needed from solution order. You need to use the FIORI app ‘Monitor Revenue Contracts’ and ‘Event-based Revenue Recognition’ for different processes to review the information 
  • Real Time Activity: You will see how the real time postings happen automatically when related operation processes such as good issue, billing and time confirmation is triggered. You don’t need to run any apps to trigger real time postings in the process.  
  • Period End Activity: At the end, you will run a period end activity ‘Run Revenue Recognition - Revenue Contracts' to post recursive revenue and review the result in ‘Display Line Items in Journal Entries’ 

Now let’s start each part in detail. 

Solution Order Setup and Related Revenue Contract 

In this step, we use mainly the FIORI apps  

  • Manage Solution Order: to create the transaction data from the orders 
  • Monitor Revenue Contracts: to monitor the revenue accounting information, you don’t need perform any transactional activities.  

We will create a solution order with 4 follow-up documents for 4 processes 

  • Sales Order item 
  • Customer Project 
  • Service Contract item 
  • Subscription Billing item

As you can see, sales order item, service contract item and subscription billing item create the following documents after the items are released. The customer project will be created later. 

Before the customer project billing plan is created, we can already review the revenue contract and corresponding performance obligations.   

You can go to the Fiori App ‘Monitor Revenue Contracts’, and you will be able to search the revenue contract per the solution order.

Note:  it is important to remember that revenue contracts are only created for US GAAP or IFRS accounting principles. No revenue contracts are created in the local accounting principles.  

In the next screen, you will be able to see the contractual price, the standalone selling price and allocated amount. 

As you see that each rate element of the subscription billing creates a performance obligation. This is configurable depending on the product name and charge type. 

So far we see POBs from sales order item, service contract item and rate elements of subscription billing. We now create the billing plan customer project.  

You first maintain the work package and assign role/resource in the customer project as usual. Then the billing information, you need to: 

  • Select the contract type and product that you have configured to ‘Use Revenue Contracts’ 
  • A new column SSP is a mandatory field, if you don’t see them, you can add them from the field repository to the list. You capture the SSP manually. 

     

  • In the end, you maintain the billing item

     

You change the status of the project to execution 

If you look at the sales order from the project, you will notice that the sales order has PSAS as the manual captured SSP, and it doesn’t use the pricing procedure of solution order, and you don’t need to add PSAS to the pricing procedure of solution order.  

And once the customer project has billing plan information and a related sales order is created, the POB is created and added to the existing revenue contracts. 

Let’s have a short summarization here. 

Each item of the solution order creates a POB (Performance Obligation) in the accounting perspective. All POBs are combined into one Revenue Contract. 

As the follow-up documents are created from the solution order in an asynchronized way, the POBs are created and combined into one revenue contract also in an asynchronized way. 

  • For Sales Order Item or Service Contract Item,  the POB is created when the sales order or service contract is created.  
  • For Subscription Billing, the POB is created when the billing plan in provider contract is created. If the follow-up subscription billing is in status “Pending’, the related POB will not be created.   Only when the subscription billing is released and the related provider contract is created, then the POB is created. 
  • For Customer Project, the POB is created when the billing plan in project is maintained and a sales order is created. 

SSP (Standalone Selling Price) is prepared using pricing (e.g., the PSAM condition for Solution Orders and Service Contracts).  

  • For Sales Order and Service Contract, it is using the pricing procedure of solution order 
  • For Subscription Billing, statistical conditions are used to carry the equivalent SSP value. 
  • For Customer Project, the SSP is defined manually.  

To know how the SSP can be configured, please refer to other blogs.

We keep operational process intact, revenue contract and related POBs are created automatically.  

 

Real Time Activities 

In this step, apart from the transactional apps to perform operational activities, such as goods issue, billing, you don’t need to perform any transactional activities in revenue recognition. The revenue adjustment and revenue recognition is triggered immediately 

Let’s break it down by scenario. We will not show the operational activities such as good issue, billing, time confirmation as it is not the focus.   

We review the postings in Fiori App ‘Display Line Items in General Ledger’ 

Sales Order Item (SellFromStock) 

  • Goods Issue (GI): Triggers realtime revenue recognition. We record revenue and COGS aligned to the delivery of goods. 
    At this moment, the revenue posted is without allocation, but the revenue due to allocation will be posted.   
  • Billing: Triggers realtime revenue adjustments (invoice corrections). For example, if billing differs in timing or value from underlying delivery, URR posts the adjustments to keep revenue accurate.

As the posting mechanism is based on the EBRR, you will see the journal entry type is RR and you can see the information with the field SLA Line Item Type which explains the nature of the postings. 

What you see in accounting: 

 

  • Immediate postings in the Universal Journal for recognized revenue and COGS at GI. 
  • Adjustment lines when the invoice posts. 
  • At real item for sales from stock, the revenue recognized is without allocation effect. You will see the revenue adjustment to due allocation at period end.

Customer Project (Fixed Price) 

  • Time Confirmation: Calculates POC (Percentage of Completion) and triggers realtime revenue recognition (matching costs and revenue per the method configured). 
  • Billing: Triggers invoicedriven adjustments (e.g., if billed vs. recognized amounts diverge at a point in time). 

What you see in accounting: 

  • Adjustment lines when the invoice posts. 
  • Revenue recognized when time confirmation is confirmed 
  • At real item for customer project, the revenue recognized is without allocation effect. You will see the revenue adjustment to due allocation at period end.

Service Contract Item (Recurring) 

  • Billing: Each cycle’s invoice triggers realtime revenue adjustment to keep contract revenue schedules aligned with actual billing. 

What you see in accounting: 

  • We will see revenue posted at period-end closing. 

Subscription Billing 

  • Billing: Same as with service contracts, billing triggers realtime revenue adjustments reflecting earned service and billed amounts. 

What you see in accounting: 

  • Adjustment lines when the invoice posts.

 

Period End Activities 

Realtime gets us far, but periodend processing ensures that everything balances and complies with accounting principles.  

We run:  

  • Revenue Recognition for Revenue Contracts for ledgers related to IFRS/USGAAP accounting principles month end (or anytime during the period if needed). 
  • Revenue Recognition for Sales Order/Project for ledger related to local accounting principles. 

Important to mention: 

There are also other period end revenue recognition activities for other objects, such as sales order, service contract and provider contract and customer project.   As you see, a revenue contract is created only in the IFRS/USGAAP accounting principle and it contains different processes such as sales order, service contract, provider contract and customer project. 

If a document is included in a revenue contract, the period activity must be run with the revenue contract. Otherwise, you will get the following warning: (We try to Run Revenue Recognition – Sales Order in ledger 2L) 

In the ledger of local accounting principle, the period end run need to run the individual document, as there is no revenue contract created. 

 

Now let’s use the Fiori App ‘Display Line Items in General Ledger’ again to see result. 

Please follow the this blog to add revenue contract ID and POB ID. 

What happens: 

Revenue Recognition Posting 

For Sales and Project items, the revenue due to allocation is now posted at period end.

For Subscription Billing/Service Contract, time-based revenue is posted.

Balance Sheet Calculation 

First of all, all items deferred revenue and accrued revenue are cleared at item level. 

Example: Accrued Revenue and Deferred Revenue of the SD Order.

Example:  Accrued Revenue and Deferred Revenue of the Service Contract.

 

Then the deferred revenue and accrued revenue from different items are further cleared against each other at revenue contract level. 

We will discuss this logic in a separate blog. 

 

Summarize the process 

In this blog, we guide you through running a simple end-2-end process using Universal Revenue Recognition in Solution Order. 

  • We start off by creating a solution order, and we review the revenue contract.  
  • We post operational activities, and review again the automatic revenue adjustment. 
  • We run period-end run to generate FI postings for time-based revenue recognition and  

The process related to accounting can be fully automate as the period-end run can be scheduled regularly.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition – All Communication Scenarios in One Place (Bookmark)
entry.content:
Make sure to bookmark this blog so you can easily revisit all communication scenarios in one place. Share it with your friends and colleagues—it’s a handy resource when searching across different SAP portals.

What is a Communication Scenario ?

A Communication Scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is a predefined, packaged set of:

  • Inbound/Outbound APIs (OData, SOAP)
  • Business events (for event-based integration)
  • Message types
  • Authorizations

These scenarios act as the integration between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and other SAP Products or external systems.

Think of a communication scenario as a “bundle” :

These are the APIs and permissions this external system is allowed to use, and here’s how the communication should happen.

Each Communication Scenario is delivered by SAP S/4HANA Public Product Release and cannot be modified—but you can activate and use them.

Key Components of a Communication Scenario

A typical scenario includes:

1. Communication User

A technical user used for authentication.
Supports:

  • Basic Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 (strongly recommended)

2. Communication System

Represents the external (non‑SAP) or SAP system you are integrating with.
Defines:

  • Hostname / Token URLs
  • Outbound target URLs
  • Authentication method

3. Communication Arrangement

This is where everything comes together.

You assign:

  • The Communication Scenario
  • The Communication System
  • The Communication User
  • Any specific parameters (e.g., service URLs, outbound destinations)

If you think of it simply:

Scenario = Template
Arrangement = Active Configuration

Why Are Communication Scenarios Important?

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, customers cannot create custom communication scenarios like in on‑premise or Private Cloud. This strict governance ensures:

  • Security and compliance
  • System stability
  • Standardized integration patterns
  • Ease of lifecycle management during upgrades

So instead of customizing scenarios, you configure and connect the right SAP‑delivered scenarios.

APIs usually come with a predefined communication scenario. To use the APIs, you first need to create a communication arrangement based on the communication scenario.

How to Configure a Communication Scenario: Step-by-Step

Step 1: Create a Communication User

SAP Fiori App: Maintain Communication Users

  • Create user → assign password → store securely
  • Or create an OAuth technical user (preferred)

Step 2: Create a Communication System

SAP Fiori App: Communication Systems

Here you:

  • Assign the communication user
  • Maintain authentication method
  • Provide target host details
  • Configure OAuth token endpoint (if applicable)

Step 3: Create Communication Arrangement

SAP Fiori App: Communication Arrangements

Steps:

  1. Select the communication scenario (e.g., SAP_COM_0008)
  2. Assign the communication system
  3. Review/activate inbound and outbound services
  4. Copy Service URLs for external system use

Once activated, the APIs are ready for use.

Testing Your Scenario

You can test APIs using tools like:

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub (API Hub)
  • SAP Integration Suite (CPI)
  • SAP API Management
  • SAP BTP  (Destinations, Side by Side Extensions, Extensibility Development)
  • Custom integrations

Check which communication scenario fits to your purposes best.

The following list gives you an overview of the most important communication scenarios available today

entry.title: Re: RCM - Email notification to the candidate when a fiel...
entry.content:
The notification should be triggered by a status change, not by a field change.

You should be able to achieve this by creating a rule that updates the status when a specific field has the value X.

If a notification is linked to that specific status the notification will be sent to the roles ( Rec Operators ) defined on the status level
